items.question: What is the difference between a function declaration and a function expression?
items.answer: Declarations are hoisted entirely, so they can be called before they appear in source. Expressions are assigned to a variable and are only available after that line executes.

items.question: How do arrow functions differ from regular functions?
items.answer: Arrow functions have no own this/arguments/prototype and cannot be used as constructors. They inherit this lexically from the enclosing scope, which makes them great for callbacks.
